text.title: Agency-to-SaaS Readiness Checklist Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed across deployments. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Client claims joint ownership of SaaS codebase mid-diligence; deal stalls.
antipattern.root-cause: Original engagement had no licence-back clause; IP rights unclear.
antipattern.fix: Audit candidate codebases for explicit licence-back / WFA assignment; exclude unclear lineage.

antipattern.symptom: SaaS launches; production hardening absent; first 10 customers churn on bugs.
antipattern.root-cause: Code came from a single engagement; never battle-tested across customers.
antipattern.fix: Require ≥3 paying engagements on the asset before pivot; defer otherwise.

antipattern.symptom: Founder out of cash month 9; back to agency work at 80% discount.
antipattern.root-cause: Pivot funded by debt + retainer burn; not 18-month covered.
antipattern.fix: Require 18-month operating cost in committed retainer; flag debt-funded pivots red.

antipattern.symptom: SaaS at <30% founder time; competitor with full-time team eats lunch.
antipattern.root-cause: Founder kept agency at 70% of week; SaaS time intermittent.
antipattern.fix: Commit ≥50% founder time or reclassify as 'side project' not 'pivot'.
